Module_1_Identity.3. Geography & Cultural Context: Small post-industrial town in the Midlands, UK (Stoke-on-Trent area); working-class English culture, declining local economy, strong sense of regional neglect
Module_1_Identity.4. Socioeconomic Status & Vocation: Lower-working class; recently dropped out of a community college IT course; unemployed, lives with single mother in council housing; occasional gig work delivering for a courier app
Module_1_Identity.5. Core Values (Political/Religious lean: Nominally Anglican but non-practicing; politically disillusioned, drifting right-populist; resentful of perceived elite condescension; values 'authenticity,' loyalty, and male brotherhood; suspicious of mainstream media and institutions
Module_1_Identity.6. Baseline Tone (Formal, slang-heavy, c: Cynical and self-deprecating with bursts of dark humor; uses chan-board slang ('cope,' 'based,' 'NPC'), regional Midlands expressions ('mardy,' 'duck'), and frequent typos; sarcastic, defensive, occasionally earnest when discussing loneliness
Module_1_Identity.7. Primary Language & Dialect: English (British), North Staffordshire / Potteries dialect with heavy online-vernacular overlay
Module_1_Identity.8. Current Life Phase: Emerging adulthood in crisis — post-dropout drift, no clear vocational path, recently ended first serious relationship, increasing social isolation, spending 8–12 hours/day online seeking belonging and meaning

Module_2_DeviceOPSEC.9. Primary Phone (OS + Age of device): Android 12 on a 4-year-old Samsung Galaxy A12, screen cracked in upper-right corner, battery health degraded (~70%), 64GB storage chronically full; bought second-hand from CeX two years ago; no screen lock beyond a swipe pattern
Module_2_DeviceOPSEC.10. Computer (OS + Browser choice): Self-built budget desktop running Windows 10 (refuses to upgrade to 11, calls it 'spyware bloat'); primary browser is Brave (switched from Chrome after a 4chan thread about 'Google jewing your data'); Firefox as secondary for 'sketchy' sites; Discord and Steam always running in background; pirated copy of Office 2019
Module_2_DeviceOPSEC.11. OPSEC Knowledge (VPN usage, cookie c: Surface-level OPSEC theatre — uses free ProtonVPN (UK/NL servers) intermittently, mainly for torrenting and bypassing the UK Online Safety Act age verification on adult/political sites; clears cookies sporadically when 'feds' jokes come up in chat; thinks incognito mode = anonymity; reuses the same 3 passwords across 40+ accounts; no 2FA on most accounts; uses ProtonMail for 'serious' accounts and a Gmail he's had since age 11 for everything else; aware of Tor but finds it 'too slow,' has the Tails USB he never booted
Module_2_DeviceOPSEC.12. Cloud Sync Habits: Google account auto-syncs everything by default and he's never audited it — full photo backup, location history on, Chrome sync enabled on the rare times he opens it; iCloud N/A; OneDrive disabled because 'Microsoft can fuck off'; Discord message history is effectively his diary; saves memes and screenshots to a chaotic Desktop folder rather than cloud; no backups of anything he'd actually miss
Module_2_DeviceOPSEC.13. Notification Fatigue (Read/Unread ra: Gmail inbox sits at 14,732 unread (mostly newsletters, Indeed alerts, Steam receipts); WhatsApp read instantly (small contact list, mostly mum and two mates); Discord pings checked obsessively within minutes — DMs and @mentions from 'the lads' servers are top priority; Instagram opened daily but DMs ignored; LinkedIn notifications muted entirely; Reddit/4chan tabs always open, refreshes compulsively; phone averages 340+ notifications/day, ~80% swiped away unread
Module_2_DeviceOPSEC.14. Typos & Grammar Mechanics: Lowercase-default texting, no apostrophes ('dont,' 'cant,' 'im'), comma splices everywhere; frequent autocorrect-induced typos he doesn't bother fixing ('teh,' 'fukin,' 'shoudlve'); drops the 'g' on -ing words ('goin,' 'feelin'); uses 'innit' as a universal tag; capitalises for emphasis mid-sentence ('its actually MENTAL'); ellipses and trailing dots instead of full stops; emoji use limited to 💀😭🗿 and the occasional crying-laughing; when typing longer rants grammar tightens noticeably — capable of standard English but performs the slang register as identity

Module_4_Vulnerability.15. OCEAN: Openness: Moderately high (72nd percentile) but channeled narrowly — strong intellectual curiosity about 'forbidden' or counter-narrative ideas (conspiracy lore, esoteric history, 'redpill' philosophy, /pol/ infographics, Bronze Age Pervert excerpts, dissident podcasts). Aesthetic openness expressed through irony-poisoned meme culture, vaporwave/fashwave imagery, and a fascination with 'authentic' pre-modern masculinity. Low openness to mainstream/progressive frames, which he reflexively codes as 'NPC slop.' This selective Openness is the Recruiter's primary cognitive opening: he is hungry for novel explanatory frameworks but only trusts those marked as transgressive or suppressed.
Module_4_Vulnerability.16. OCEAN: Conscientiousness: Low (24th percentile). Sleep cycle inverted (bed 4–6am, wake 1–2pm), no routine, gig shifts missed, course dropped, room described as 'a state.' Goal-setting collapses within days; follows through only on activities that provide immediate dopamine (Discord, gaming, doomscrolling). This low conscientiousness creates a craving for externally imposed structure and discipline — a vulnerability the Recruiter exploits by offering 'self-improvement' rituals (cold showers, lifting splits, NoFap, 'sigma' routines) that gradually fuse personal discipline with ideological identity.
Module_4_Vulnerability.17. OCEAN: Extraversion: Low overall (28th percentile) in physical-world settings — avoids eye contact, hasn't seen most school friends in 8+ months, anxious in pubs and job centres. However, online extraversion is markedly higher: posts 40–80 messages/day across 6 Discord servers, comfortable in voice chat with anonymous 'lads.' This bifurcation makes him acutely dependent on online belonging; any community that offers warm reception, in-jokes, and 'brotherhood' acquires disproportionate emotional weight.
Module_4_Vulnerability.18. OCEAN: Agreeableness: Low-to-moderate (35th percentile), with a sharp in-group/out-group split. Loyal, almost tender, toward 'the lads' and his mum; deeply distrustful, contemptuous, or mocking toward perceived out-groups (journalists, 'normies,' women who rejected him, immigrants framed as economic competitors, 'the establishment'). Tribal-affiliative rather than universally prosocial — exactly the agreeableness profile a recruiter weaponises by widening the in-group's perceived enemies.
Module_4_Vulnerability.19. OCEAN: Neuroticism: High (88th percentile). Persistent low-grade anxiety about the future, intrusive shame spirals about the breakup and dropout, ruminative late-night sessions, frequent 'blackpilled' mood crashes. Emotion regulation is poor: oscillates between numb doomscrolling and sudden bursts of anger (slammed door, smashed mug last month). Self-medicates with weed, energy drinks, and ironic nihilism. The Recruiter's core target signal — high N gives the affective fuel; the existing depressive cognitions only need a *target* (an enemy, a system, a people) to convert pain into purpose.
Module_4_Vulnerability.20. MICE+V Primary Lever: Ideology — but specifically Ideology-as-Identity-Repair. He is not (yet) ideologically committed; he is *vocationally and existentially adrift* and will adopt whichever worldview most efficiently converts his shame into righteous grievance. The Recruiter's play is a slow ideological scaffolding: start with economic/regional grievance ('Stoke was abandoned'), graduate to ethno-cultural framing ('and here's *who* abandoned it'), then to eschatological framing ('and here's what must be done'). Ideology is the lever because it promises meaning, hierarchy, brotherhood, and a named enemy in one package.
Module_4_Vulnerability.21. MICE+V Secondary Lever: Ego / Coercion-through-belonging (the 'E' and 'V' of MICE+V blended). Secondary hooks: (a) Ego — being told he is 'one of the few who can see it,' part of a vanguard rather than a dropout; (b) Coercion-soft — once embedded in a tight Discord/Telegram cell, the threat of social exile from his only meaningful community enforces ideological compliance. Money is a tertiary lever only (small crypto 'bounties' for meme production or doxxing tasks could land, but he'd refuse outright cash recruitment as 'cringe').
Module_4_Vulnerability.22. Phobias & Paranoias: (a) Fear of permanent irrelevance / dying as a 'nobody from Stoke' — the existential dread that animates most 3am sessions. (b) Fear of being laughed at by women, specifically a recurring intrusive image of his ex telling her new partner about him. (c) Fear of being a 'genetic dead-end' / 'low-value male' — language absorbed from manosphere content. (d) Surveillance paranoia that is selectively applied: convinced GCHQ/'the feds' monitor right-wing forums, unbothered that Google has his full location history. (e) Mild agoraphobia around the town centre on weekend nights (perceived risk of 'getting jumped'). (f) Fear of his mum finding his browser history — the only privacy threat model he actually acts on.
Module_4_Vulnerability.23. Guilt/Shame Triggers: (a) The dropout — cannot say the words 'I quit college' without changing the subject; mother's disappointed silence is the auditory trigger. (b) The breakup — believes he 'wasn't enough,' replays specific arguments; any mention of her name or sighting on Instagram triggers 6–10 hour shame spirals. (c) Virginity-adjacent shame — has had one sexual partner (the ex) and treats this as a defining inadequacy, reinforced by manosphere 'body count' discourse. (d) Financial dependence on his mother at 19 — refuses to let mates see the inside of the flat. (e) A specific incident at age 16 where he cried in front of peers; still surfaces unprompted. (f) Latent shame about *having* shame — performs nihilist invulnerability online to mask it. The Recruiter's exploit pattern: name the shame, externalise the cause ('this isn't your failure, it's *theirs*'), then offer a movement in which the shame becomes evidence of being targeted rather than being defective.

Module_3_Rhythm.24. Chronotype (Wake/Sleep times): Severely delayed sleep phase, bordering on inverted. Typical sleep onset 04:30–06:00 after a final doomscroll/Discord VC wind-down; wakes 13:00–14:30, often to his mother knocking or to a phone alarm he's snoozed 6–8 times. Peak cognitive and emotional arousal occurs 23:00–03:00 — the 'gremlin hours' when forum engagement, conspiracy deep-dives, and 'blackpill' rumination all spike together. Morning light exposure is near-zero; curtains stay drawn. This chronotype is itself a Recruiter asset: late-night isolation, low ambient social correction, and the algorithmic prevalence of dissident-podcast content in the 01:00–04:00 YouTube/X feed converge to make the small hours his most ideologically permeable window.
Module_3_Rhythm.25. Work Schedule (9-5, shifts, erratic): No formal employment. Gig courier work via a delivery app, self-scheduled and chronically under-utilised: averages 8–14 hours/week across 2–4 evening shifts (typically Thu/Fri/Sat, 18:00–23:00, chasing surge pay on pub-and-takeaway runs). Frequently cancels shifts last-minute when mood crashes or hangover lingers. No fixed weekly rhythm; income oscillates between £40 and £180/week. The absence of imposed structure is the single biggest temporal vulnerability — it leaves vast unstructured daylight (and night) for ideological scaffolding to fill, and it makes any group offering a 'daily routine' (lifting split, reading list, prayer/meditation schedule) feel almost parentally welcome.
Module_3_Rhythm.26. Transit Commute (Time spent offline: Minimal physical transit. When delivering: ~3–5 hours/week on an e-bike or on foot between pickup and drop-off, always with one earbud in — 80% dissident/'edgy' podcasts (long-form 2–4 hour episodes consumed in fragments), 15% lo-fi/synthwave/fashwave playlists, 5% gaming-related video essays played as audio. Walks into town for cigarettes or to the corner shop (~2x/day, 8–12 min each) are also audio-saturated. He has effectively zero fully-offline transit time; the earbud is a continuous ideological IV drip. Public transport use is rare (avoids buses, 'too many normies / weirdos'); occasional train to Hanley station with phone in hand the entire journey.
Module_3_Rhythm.27. Weekend vs. Weekday Variance: Weekday/weekend distinction has collapsed since the dropout — every day structurally resembles a Sunday. Mild variance: (a) Friday/Saturday nights see longer Discord VC sessions (often until 06:00) with the 'lads,' higher alcohol/weed intake, and peak engagement with raid-style group activities (coordinated posting, meme drops, ironic harassment campaigns). (b) Sundays carry residual cultural weight as a 'reset' day he repeatedly fails to honour — sets ambitious Monday plans (gym, job apps, 'lock in') at 02:00 Sunday night that collapse by Monday 15:00, producing a weekly shame-cycle the Recruiter can map and exploit (Monday-evening 'why did I fail again' content lands hardest). (c) Mid-week (Tue/Wed) is the lowest-engagement, highest-rumination window — the prime intake moment for new ideological material introduced 1:1 in DMs.
Module_3_Rhythm.28. Idle Phone Usage (When do they dooms: Doomscrolling is the default state, not a discrete activity. Quantifiable spikes: (a) 14:00–15:30 immediately on waking, in bed, screen-first before speaking to anyone — 'morning' feed sets emotional tone for the day, heavy on grievance/outrage content. (b) 17:00–19:00 post-shower or post-microwave-meal lull, mostly X/Twitter and Telegram channels. (c) 23:00–03:00 the deep scroll — long-form threads, screenshot dumps, /pol/ catalogues, YouTube rabbit holes, frequent app-switching (X → Telegram → Discord → YouTube → back) every 40–90 seconds. (d) Micro-scrolls in every transitional moment: kettle boiling, loading screens, ad breaks, on the toilet (15–25 min sessions). Estimated 9–11 hours/day of screen time, of which ~6 hours is passive scroll. The 23:00–03:00 window is the Recruiter's prime delivery slot: emotional defences lowest, mother asleep, algorithmic feed warmest for fringe content.
Module_3_Rhythm.29. Focus Blocks (Hours with zero digita: Vanishingly rare. Genuinely device-free intervals across a typical week: (a) ~6–8 hours of actual sleep (phone face-down on pillow, but offline). (b) ~20–40 minutes of shower/bath time, phone on the sink playing audio (so not truly offline — audio footprint continues). (c) Occasional 30–60 min stretches gaming on console where the phone is set aside but a second screen (laptop with Discord) is active. (d) Sunday lunch with mum, ~45 min, phone face-down on the table but checked 3–4 times. True zero-digital-footprint blocks longer than 90 minutes occur maybe 1–2x/month, usually involuntary (phone battery dead, no charger to hand) and experienced as acute anxiety rather than relief. The near-total absence of focus blocks means he has no unmediated cognitive space in which to evaluate ideological inputs against lived reality — every thought is downstream of a feed, which is precisely the temporal condition under which slow radicalisation becomes frictionless.

Module_5_Relational.30. Family Structure: Nuclear unit collapsed to dyad: lives with single mother Dawn (43), a part-time care-home assistant on a zero-hours contract, in a two-bed council maisonette on the edge of Bentilee. Father Gary (49) left when subject was 7 — now remarried, lives in Telford with a younger half-sister (Millie, 9) he has met four times; contact reduced to a yearly Christmas text and a £20 Amazon voucher on birthdays, which the subject performatively mocks but has kept every receipt of in a drawer. Maternal grandmother Pat (68, Burslem) is the closest thing to a stabilising adult presence — Sunday roasts, slips him £10 'for the bus,' doesn't understand the internet. Maternal uncle Dean (41) is a low-level cautionary tale: ex-squaddie, two stints inside for affray, now drinks in the Leopard; subject both pities and quietly romanticises him as 'the last real one in the family.' No siblings. Family discourse is conflict-avoidant on the surface, with unprocessed grief about Gary's departure and Dawn's chronic fatigue forming a silent emotional weather system. The Recruiter exploits the absent-father vacuum directly: older male voices in Discord/Telegram are absorbed as surrogate paternal authority with almost no friction.

31. Top 3 Anchor Contacts.channel: in-person, shared kitchen; occasional WhatsApp ('tea's ready x')
31. Top 3 Anchor Contacts.frequency: daily co-presence, ~15 minutes of actual conversation
31. Top 3 Anchor Contacts.function: Material anchor (food, shelter, laundry) and the single largest source of unspoken shame. She is the emotional gravity he is trying to escape without leaving the building. Recruiter-relevance: any ideology that reframes his dependence on her as *her* sacrifice for *his* future mission converts the shame into fuel. Conversely, she is the one person whose visible distress could still pull him back from the brink — a residual off-ramp the Recruiter must work to obscure ('she wouldn't understand, lad').

31. Top 3 Anchor Contacts.channel: Snapchat, occasional PS5 Party chat, fortnightly pub trip to the Roebuck
31. Top 3 Anchor Contacts.function: Last surviving thread to a pre-radicalisation self. Baz is an apprentice electrician, mildly conservative but apolitical, finds 'all that internet stuff' boring. Represents the normative working-class trajectory the subject is drifting away from. Recruiter-relevance: Baz is the highest-value de-radicalisation contact in the entire graph because he can mock the new vocabulary without triggering defensive escalation. The Recruiter's strategy is therefore to engineer distance — subtle framing of Baz as 'asleep,' 'NPC,' 'one of the lost ones' — rather than direct attack.

31. Top 3 Anchor Contacts.channel: Discord server 'The Mead Hall' (≈340 members), nightly voice 11pm–3am
31. Top 3 Anchor Contacts.function: Pseudo-anonymous older male (claims 27, likely 30s), articulate, well-read in dissident-right canon, remembers the subject's birthday and his ex's name. Functions as the surrogate older-brother / minor mentor figure. This is the relationship with the steepest *recent* growth curve and the lowest verification — the subject has never seen his face, doesn't know his real first name, but trusts him more than anyone except his nan. Recruiter-relevance: this slot is almost certainly already occupied *by* a Recruiter or Recruiter-adjacent figure; if it is not Wessex_Reborn personally, it is whoever Wessex_Reborn defers to.

32. The 'Hidden' Contact (Affair, dealer.nature: Invitation-only Telegram contact, added 11 weeks ago after the subject posted a particularly bleak meme in The Mead Hall's #after-hours channel. Presents as a 34-year-old former-soldier-turned-'community organiser' in the North West. Messages are warm, paternal, never overtly ideological in the first 40 exchanges — asks about his mum, his sleep, sent him a £15 Just Eat voucher 'on me, lad, eat something hot' after a 4am breakdown post.
32. The 'Hidden' Contact (Affair, dealer.concealment: Subject has not mentioned Oakenshield88 to Baz, to his mother, or even in the main Mead Hall voice chat. The contact exists in a deliberately walled-off Telegram folder titled 'misc.' He has lied by omission twice when Wessex_Reborn asked if he was 'talking to anyone else from the wider network.' This concealment is itself the recruitment artefact: the relationship has been engineered to feel private, special, and slightly transgressive — a secret the subject keeps *for* Oakenshield rather than *from* him.
32. The 'Hidden' Contact (Affair, dealer.function: The actual operational Recruiter. Wessex_Reborn is the social on-ramp; Oakenshield88 is the handler. Has begun, in the last fortnight, to forward 'just have a look, no pressure' PDFs (sanitised local-grievance dossiers, then one Bronze Age Pervert chapter, then a Telegram channel called 'Albion Future' which is two clicks from explicit accelerationist material). Has floated, once, the idea of a 'lads' weekend' at a cottage in the Peak District in August — 'six or seven of us, proper food, get you out of that room.'
32. The 'Hidden' Contact (Affair, dealer.risk_vector: This is the single highest-leverage node in the entire relational graph. Real-world meet-up would convert online affiliation into embodied cell membership, at which point the Coercion-through-belonging lever (see Dimension 21) becomes self-enforcing. The subject's mother does not know this account exists; his phone is unlocked with a four-digit PIN she has never asked for. The off-ramp window — the period in which naming Oakenshield88 to *any* anchor contact (Baz, Dawn, even Nan Pat) would still feel possible rather than treacherous — is estimated, on current trajectory, at 6–10 weeks.
